action.description: Create LeadRecord + SQL upsert for contacts table

action.name: rankPillars

action.name: trackAcquisitionEvent

action.description: Track social event

trigger.description: Subscription state machine transitions into canceled (terminal)

action.name: markContactChurned
action.description: Mark crm.contacts.lifecycle_stage=churned + log a churn activity. Resolves contact via subscription.deal_source_ref → crm.deals.contact_id, falling back to customers.contact_source_ref.

trigger.description: Subscription transition that changes the tenant MRR (new-sub / churn / upgrade / downgrade)

action.name: emitRevenueDeltaEvent
action.description: Emit a typed revenue.mrr.delta event for the analytics-forge ingest path. Pure event payload — analytics owns persistence + bucketing.

trigger.description: Cadence-runner about to send a touch with attachDemo:true; needs a per-lead preview spawn (Plan 6 — the AllOnce moat)

action.name: spawnMinimalBrief
action.description: Trigger brand-forge spawn in DEMO_MODE: brand-name + palette + wordmark + site-hero + one section + tokens; output lands at <tenantId>/hunter/demos/<lead-slug>/

trigger.description: Brand-forge demo spawn finished; preview URL ready (round-trip with bridge-75)

action.name: attachDemoUrl
action.description: Write leads.demo_url so the next cadence pass can interpolate it into the touch body. Lead-slug parsed from the spawn outputPrefix.

trigger.description: A lead's outreach culminated in a paid checkout — conversion row links acquisition cost → revenue

action.name: recordAttribution
action.description: Attach lead-source + acquisition-cost provenance to the payment_intent / subscription row. Includes daysToConvert derivation. Informational, not blocking — payment-forge already processed the charge through Stripe webhook.

trigger.description: Lead-hunter scored above threshold AND deduper confirmed new contact (fan-out target alongside bridge-60 lead → email-forge)

action.name: upsertContact
action.description: Write a row in crm.contacts with provenance pointing back to the lead row. UPSERT key (tenant_id, source_name, source_external_id) per crm-pusher config. Falls back to <source>:<leadId> external id when source didn't supply one.

trigger.description: Same objection theme surfaced N times across replies in the analysis window — pattern signal that one content piece would address it once-and-for-all

action.name: draftObjectionResponse
action.description: Content-factory drafts a piece (blog-post / faq-entry / sales-card / tweet-thread depending on theme); operator approves before publish. Format picked from theme; priority from count (≥10 → high, ≥4 → normal, else low).

trigger.description: Lead got N email touches with no reply but score still warm — usually means email is wrong channel; warm public engagement on social can break silence

action.name: engageOnSocial
action.description: Schedule a low-touch action on a resolvable social profile (LinkedIn > X > Instagram priority). 1-2 touches → like-recent-post; 3-4 → comment-on-recent-post when warm; 5+ → connection-request. NEVER auto-DM (separate adapter with consent gating). All actions require operator ratify.

trigger.description: CRM is system-of-record for do-not-contact decisions made outside lead-hunter (operator marked in HubSpot, customer emailed support@, Mailchimp unsubscribe sync, Salesforce DNC flag)

action.name: syncSuppression
action.description: Mirror the suppression so the next cadence-runner pass blocks any send. Source maps to SuppressionReason: operator-manual stays itself; mailchimp/support → unsubscribe; salesforce/crm-sync/unknown → unknown. Address lower-cased on the way in for store key consistency.

trigger.description: Subscription cancelled — voluntary churn, involuntary (failed payment), or admin (refund/fraud/ToS). Social campaigns that re-engage churned customers (testimonial requests, "look how happy our customers are" posts, upsell drips) need to skip that contact for a cool-down window so the brand voice stays consistent with the actual relationship state.

action.name: skipAudienceSegment
action.description: Compute a skip-audience instruction for the social-forge campaign runner: contactId + skipUntil + skipSegmentTags. Tags depend on cancellation reason — voluntary (30d) skips testimonial/upsell/community; involuntary (7d) skips testimonial/spotlight only (recovery campaigns still run); admin (60d) skips everything brand-feel-good. Pure-compute helper at src/lib/bridges/payment-to-social.ts.

contributors.contribution: brand images, email headers, video clips

rules.name: Throttle email edges to warmup ceiling
Skip dunning if payment received within 24h
Alert operator on critical bridge failure
